sections.label: Section 792
sections.original: τοὔργον μὲν ἦν τοῦτʼ , ὄνομα δʼ οὐκ εἶχεν τόδε .
sections.translation: That was the deed , though it did not have that name .

sections.label: Section 793
sections.original: πάντʼ οἶσθʼ ἄρʼ , ὡς ἔοικας , ἀμφʼ ἐμῶν γάμων .
sections.translation: Then you know everything , it seems , about my marriage .

sections.label: Section 794
sections.original: οἶδʼ · εἰ δὲ λέκτρα διέφυγες τάδʼ οὐκ ἔχω .
sections.translation: I do . But if you have escaped his bed — that I do not know .

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: ἄθικτον εὐνὴν ἴσθι σοι σεσῳσμένην .
sections.translation: Know that I have saved myself untouched for you .

sections.label: Section 796
sections.original: τίς τοῦδε πειθώ ; φίλα γάρ , εἰ σαφῆ λέγεις .
sections.translation: What could persuade me of this ? If true , your words are sweet .

sections.label: Section 797
sections.original: ὁρᾷς τάφου τοῦδʼ ἀθλίους ἕδρας ἐμάς ;
sections.translation: Do you see my wretched sanctuary at this tomb ?

sections.label: Section 798
sections.original: ὁρῶ ταλαίνας στιβάδας , ὧν τί σοὶ μέτα ;
sections.translation: I see a miserable bed of straw , but what do you have to do with it ?

sections.label: Section 799
sections.original: ἐνταῦθα λέκτρων ἱκετεύομεν φυγάς .
sections.translation: Here , as a suppliant , I am asking for an escape from his bed .

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: βωμοῦ σπανίζουσʼ ἢ νόμοισι βαρβάροις ;
sections.translation: For want of an altar , or because it is the barbarians ’ way ?

sections.label: Section 801
sections.original: ἐρρύεθʼ ἡμᾶς τοῦτʼ ἴσον ναοῖς θεῶν .
sections.translation: This was as good a protection to me as the temples of the gods .

sections.label: Section 802
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἄρα πρὸς οἴκους ναυστολεῖν σʼ ἔξεστί μοι ;
sections.translation: Then it ’ s not possible for me to take you home by ship ?

sections.label: Section 803
sections.original: ξίφος μένει σε μᾶλλον ἢ τοὐμὸν λέχος .
sections.translation: A sword is waiting for you , rather than my bed .

sections.label: Section 804
sections.original: οὕτως ἂν εἴην ἀθλιώτατος βροτῶν .
sections.translation: So I would be the most wretched of mortals .

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: μή νυν καταιδοῦ , φεῦγε δʼ ἐκ τῆσδε χθονός .
sections.translation: Do not feel shame now , but escape from this land .

sections.label: Section 806
sections.original: λιπών σε ; Τροίαν ἐξέπερσα σὴν χάριν .
sections.translation: Leaving you behind ? I ravaged Troy for your sake .

sections.label: Section 807
sections.original: κρεῖσσον γὰρ ἤ σε τἄμʼ ἀποκτεῖναι λέχη .
sections.translation: Yes , for that is better than that our union should cause your death .

sections.label: Section 808
sections.original: ἄνανδρά γʼ εἶπας Ἰλίου τʼ οὐκ ἄξια .
sections.translation: Oh ! these are coward ’ s words , unworthy of those days at Troy .

sections.label: Section 809
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν κτάνοις τύραννον , ὃ σπεύδεις ἴσως .
sections.translation: You could not kill the tyrant , your possible intention .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: οὕτω σιδήρῳ τρωτὸν οὐκ ἔχει δέμας ;
sections.translation: Does he then have a body that cannot be wounded by a sword ?

sections.label: Section 811
sections.original: εἴσῃ . τὸ τολμᾶν δʼ ἀδύνατʼ ἀνδρὸς οὐ σοφοῦ .
sections.translation: You will hear . But to undertake impossibilities is no mark of wisdom .

sections.label: Section 812
sections.original: σιγῇ παράσχω δῆτʼ ἐμὰς δῆσαι χέρας ;
sections.translation: And so I am to offer my hands to be bound , in silence ?

sections.label: Section 813
sections.original: ἐς ἄπορον ἥκεις · δεῖ δὲ μηχανῆς τινος .
sections.translation: You are in a dilemma ; we need some contrivance .

sections.label: Section 814
sections.original: δρῶντας γὰρ ἢ μὴ δρῶντας ἥδιον θανεῖν .
sections.translation: Yes , for it is sweeter to die in action than by not acting .

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: μίʼ ἔστιν ἐλπίς , ᾗ μόνῃ σωθεῖμεν ἄν .
sections.translation: There is one hope , and only one , for our safety .

sections.label: Section 816
sections.original: ὠνητὸς ἢ τολμητὸς ἢ λόγων ὕπο ;
sections.translation: Are we to buy it , or dare it , or win it with words ?

sections.label: Section 817
sections.original: εἰ μὴ τύραννός σʼ ἐκπύθοιτʼ ἀφιγμένον .
sections.translation: If the tyrant were not to learn of your arrival .

sections.label: Section 818
sections.original: ἐρεῖ δὲ τίς μʼ ; οὐ γνώσεταί γʼ ὅς εἰμʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Will any one tell him about me ? He will certainly not know who I am .

sections.label: Section 819
sections.original: ἔστʼ ἔνδον αὐτῷ ξύμμαχος θεοῖς ἴση .
sections.translation: He has within an ally equal to the gods .

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: φήμη τις οἴκων ἐν μυχοῖς ἱδρυμένη ;
sections.translation: A voice that has settled in the inmost parts of his house ?

sections.label: Section 821
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλʼ ἀδελφή · Θεονόην καλοῦσί νιν .
sections.translation: No , but his sister ; she is called Theonoe .

sections.label: Section 822
sections.original: χρηστήριον μὲν τοὔνομʼ · ὅ τι δὲ δρᾷ φράσον .
sections.translation: The name is prophetic ; tell me what she does .

sections.label: Section 823
sections.original: πάντʼ οἶδʼ , ἐρεῖ τε συγγόνῳ παρόντα σε .
sections.translation: She knows everything , and she will tell her brother that you are here .

sections.label: Section 824
sections.original: θνῄσκοιμεν ἄν · λαθεῖν γὰρ οὐχ οἷόν τέ μοι .
sections.translation: We must die ; for I cannot escape her notice .

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: ἴσως ἂν ἀναπείσαιμεν ἱκετεύοντέ νιν —
sections.translation: Perhaps we might persuade her by supplication —

sections.label: Section 826
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δρᾶσαι ; τίνʼ ὑπάγεις μʼ ἐς ἐλπίδα ;
sections.translation: To do what ? What hope are you leading me to ?

sections.label: Section 827
sections.original: παρόντα γαίᾳ μὴ φράσαι σε συγγόνῳ .
sections.translation: Not to tell her brother that you are here in this land .

sections.label: Section 828
sections.original: πείσαντε δʼ ἐκ γῆς διορίσαιμεν ἂν πόδα ;
sections.translation: If we persuade her , could we get away from this country ?

sections.label: Section 829
sections.original: κοινῇ γʼ ἐκείνῃ ῥᾳδίως , λάθρᾳ δʼ ἂν οὔ .
sections.translation: Easily , in common with her ; but secretly we could not .

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: σὸν ἔργον , ὡς γυναικὶ πρόσφορον γυνή .
sections.translation: The task is yours ; it is suitable for women to deal with women .

sections.label: Section 831
sections.original: ὡς οὐκ ἄχρωστα γόνατʼ ἐμῶν ἕξει χερῶν .
sections.translation: Be sure that I will clasp her knees .

sections.label: Section 832
sections.original: φέρʼ , ἢν δὲ δὴ νῷν μὴ ἀποδέξηται λόγους ;
sections.translation: Well , then , what if she rejects our proposals ?

sections.label: Section 833
sections.original: θανῇ · γαμοῦμαι δʼ ἡ τάλαινʼ ἐγὼ βίᾳ .
sections.translation: You will die . And I , the unhappy one , will be married by force .

sections.label: Section 834
sections.original: προδότις ἂν εἴης · τὴν βίαν σκήψασʼ ἔχεις .
sections.translation: You would be a traitor ; that force of yours is all an excuse .

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἁγνὸν ὅρκον σὸν κάρα κατώμοσα ⋯
sections.translation: But I have sworn a sacred oath by your life —

sections.label: Section 836
sections.original: τί φῄς ; θανεῖσθαι ; κοὔποτʼ ἀλλάξεις λέχη ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ? To die ? And never to take his bed in exchange for mine ?

sections.label: Section 837
sections.original: ταὐτῷ ξίφει γε · κείσομαι δὲ σοῦ πέλας .
sections.translation: Yes , by the same sword ; I will lie at your side .

sections.label: Section 838
sections.original: ἐπὶ τοῖσδε τοίνυν δεξιᾶς ἐμῆς θίγε .
sections.translation: Then on these conditions touch my right hand .

sections.label: Section 839
sections.original: ψαύω , θανόντος σοῦ τόδʼ ἐκλείψειν φάος .
sections.translation: I touch it , swearing that I will leave the light of day if you die .

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: κἀγὼ στερηθεὶς σοῦ τελευτήσειν βίον .
sections.translation: And I will end my life if I lose you .

sections.label: Section 841
sections.original: πῶς οὖν θανούμεθʼ ὥστε καὶ δόξαν λαβεῖν ;
sections.translation: How then shall we die so as to insure our reputation for this ?

sections.label: Section 842
sections.original: τύμβου ʼπὶ νώτῳ σὲ κτανὼν ἐμὲ κτενῶ .
sections.translation: I will kill you on the tomb ’ s surface , and then kill myself . But first I will fight a great contest for your bed . Let anyone who wishes come near !

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: τὸ Τρωικὸν γὰρ οὐ καταισχυνῶ κλέος
sections.translation: For I will not disgrace my Trojan fame , nor , on my return to Hellas , will I receive great blame — I who robbed Thetis of Achilleus , and saw the slaughter of Aias , son of Telamon , and the son of Neleus made childless ;

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: οὐκ ἀξιώσω κατθανεῖν δάμαρτʼ ἐγώ ;
sections.translation: shall I not resolve to die for my wife ? Most certainly ; for if the gods are wise , they lightly bury in the earth a brave man who has been killed by his enemies , while cowards they cast up out of the earth onto a harsh rock .

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: ὦ θεοί , γενέσθω δή ποτʼ εὐτυχὲς γένος
sections.translation: O gods , may the race of Tantalos be fortunate at last , and may it be set free from evils !

sections.label: Section 857
sections.original: οἲ ἐγὼ τάλαινα · τῆς τύχης γὰρ ὧδʼ ἔχω .
sections.translation: Ah , I am unhappy , for so is my fate ! Menelaos , we are destroyed . The prophetess Theonoe is coming out of the house ; it resounds as the

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: κλῄθρων λυθέντων . φεῦγʼ · ἀτὰρ τί φευκτέον ;
sections.translation: bolts are unfastened . Try to escape ! But what is the use of trying ? For whether she is absent or present she knows of your arrival here . Oh , I am lost , unfortunate ! Saved from Troy and from a barbarian land , you have come only to fall upon barbarian swords .

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: ἡγοῦ σύ μοι φέρουσα λαμπτήρων σέλας
sections.translation: Please lead the way with blazing torches , and purify , according to the sacred law , the inmost corners of the air , so I may receive the pure breath of heaven ; and you in turn , if someone has harmed the path by treading with unholy foot , submit it to the cleansing fire ,

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: κροῦσον δὲ πεύκην , ἵνα διεξέλθω , πυρός .
sections.translation: and strike the torch in front of me , so that I may pass through . And when you have paid back to the gods my customary observance , take the household flame within .

sections.label: Section 873
sections.original: Ἑλένη , τί τἀμὰ — πῶς ἔχει — θεσπίσματα ;
sections.translation: Helen , what about my prophecy — how is it ? This man , your husband Menelaos , has openly arrived ,

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: νεῶν στερηθεὶς τοῦ τε σοῦ μιμήματος .
sections.translation: robbed of his ships and of your counterfeit . O unhappy man ! What troubles you have escaped to come here ; nor do you know whether you are to return home or to stay here . For there will be strife among the gods , and a solemn assembly held by Zeus on your account this very day .
